name: Animator

values.name: alongWith
values.comment:
Most general combination of two `Animation`s.

You probably want to narrow this down for your specific domain.
values.type: (Basics.Float -> a -> b -> c) -> Animator.Animation a -> Animator.Animation b -> Animator.Animation c

values.name: andAfter
values.comment:
Takes two animations and plays them one after the other.

The order here is such that it's convenient when using a pipe:

    beforAnimation
        |> andAfter afterAnimation
values.type: Animator.Animation a -> Animator.Animation a -> Animator.Animation a

values.name: clampAnim
values.comment:
Force the animation's initial and final values to what they would be at the start and end.

By default, animations can "leak" out of their bounds. This makes it so that when the animation is rendered "beyond" its bounds, it "holds" the value it ends with.

values.name: cutAt
values.comment:
Effectively sets the duration of the animation to be shorter.

This might not behave how you expect it: animations can keep going past their end, you might want to use `freezeAfter` if you want the animation to actually stop.

values.name: delay
values.comment: Delays the start of an animation by the given time

values.name: duration
values.comment: Get the duration of an existing animation

values.name: endWith
values.comment: Force the animation's final value to the given argument

values.name: fitIn
values.comment: Speeds up the animation to fit within the given time

values.name: freezeAfter
values.comment: Stops the animation after the given time.

values.name: getEndState
values.comment: Get the final state of an existing animation

values.name: loopForever
values.comment: Sets an duration of `Infinity` and forever loops the animation

values.name: mapRes
values.comment: Modifies the output of the animation

values.name: oobValue
values.comment: Force the animation's initial and final values to the given argument

values.name: render
values.comment: Actually "runs" the animation at the given time.

values.name: reverse
values.comment: Reverse the animation entirely

values.name: sequence
values.comment: With an initial value, plays a list of `Animation` in sequence.

values.name: speedup
values.comment: Speeds up the animation by a desired factor

values.name: startWith
values.comment: Force the animation's initial value to the given argument

values.name: together
values.comment:
Plays a list of `Animation`s together.

Almost certainly more useful narrowed to a specific domain. Use `mapRes` to turn `List a` into something more helpful.

values.name: withDuration
values.comment: Most general constructor for an `Animation`.

values.name: withNoBounds
values.comment: Create a constant animation of 0-duration

values.name: withUnitBounds
values.comment: Probably what wants to be used by default, expects an animation from 0 to 1.

name: Animator.Math
comment:
Small math helpers for making animations.

@docs modByFloat, sign, interp

values.name: interp
values.comment:
Linearly interpolates between the first two numbers.

    -- interp start end 0 == start
    -- interp start end 1 == end

    interp 0 10 0.3 == 3
    interp 0 100 -2 == -200

values.name: modByFloat
values.comment:
Takes a modulo, but is accepting of floats.

    modByFloat 3.0 9.1 == 0.1

values.name: sign
values.comment:
Returns the sign of the number

    sign 0 == 0
    sign 3 == 1
    sign -5 == -1
